paper_title: Proximate, Sugar, and Amino Acid Analysis of Bee Pollen

methods.description: Process for analyzing the proximate compositions of water, protein, lipid, and ash in bee pollen samples to determine their nutritional content.

steps.description: The fresh bee pollen samples (about 200 g) were drying in an oven at 40°C for at least 24 h until a constant weight was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: dry bee pollen samples in an oven to constant weight

steps.description: The water content was calculated by comparing the weight of fresh and dehydrated bee pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate water content from weight difference

steps.description: The protein content was determined using the Kjedahl method, and the total protein content was obtained by multiplying nitrogen values by a conversion factor of 6.25.
steps.searchable_summary: determine protein content using Kjedahl method

steps.description: The lipid content was determined using a Soxhlet extractor with diethyl ether as the solvent.
steps.searchable_summary: determine lipid content using Soxhlet extraction

steps.description: The ash content was measured using the dry combustion method in a 600°C furnace.
steps.searchable_summary: measure ash content using dry combustion

steps.description: The carbohydrate content was calculated as follows: carbohydrate content = 100 − (g of protein + g of lipid + g of ash).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate carbohydrate content from other components

steps.description: Total energy was calculated as follows: energy (kcal/g) = 5.65 × (g of protein) + 4.1 × (g of carbohydrate) + 9.3 × (g of lipid).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total energy from macronutrient content

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the sugar profile of bee pollen using high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) with an evaporative light scattering detector.

steps.description: Bee pollen (1 g) was ground in liquid nitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: grind bee pollen in liquid nitrogen

steps.description: The pollen powder was ultrasonicated with 20 mL 50% ethanol solution for 20 min, then shaken for 10 min and centrifuged for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: ultrasonicate and centrifuge pollen powder in ethanol

steps.description: The supernatant was filtered through 0.22-µm filter paper.
10 µL of the filtered sample was loaded into the ZORBAX NH2 column with 75% acetonitrile as the mobile phase at a flow rate of 1.0 mL/min.
steps.searchable_summary: load sample into HPLC column with acetonitrile

steps.description: The sugar peaks were identified by comparing the retention times obtained from the reference standards.
steps.searchable_summary: identify sugar peaks by retention time comparison

steps.description: The sugar content in bee pollen was calculated according to the standard curves made by each reference sugar.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sugar content using standard curves

methods.description: Method for analyzing the total amino acid content in bee pollen using reverse-phased liquid chromatography.

steps.description: Bee pollen (100 mg) defatted with petroleum ether was hydrolyzed with 1 mL of 4 N CH3SO3H containing 0.2% tryptamine for 72 h at 115°C in 25-mL vacuum-sealed ampules.
steps.searchable_summary: hydrolyze defatted bee pollen with CH3SO3H

steps.description: The pH of the cooled and filtered hydrolysate was adjusted.
Analyzed using a reverse-phased liquid chromatography equipped with a UV detector.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze amino acids using reverse-phased liquid chromatography

steps.description: Eighteen amino acids were quantified by comparing peak areas with those of standard amino acids.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify amino acids by peak area comparison
